tweets.content: RT by @elonmusk: Only AI can keep up with AI. That's what will guide the entire security model of the next decade.
tweets.translated: RT @elonmusk：只有人工智能才能跟上人工智能的步伐。这将指导未来十年的整个安全模型。

RT by @cz_binance: Spotted some bánh mì? 🥖 We know that look. 😋 Good news if you're traveling in Vietnam: you can pay with crypto. Just scan the local QR code with Binance Pay and grab your food to go. Here's how → https://www.binance.com/events/scan-and-pay-tourist-payment?utm_source=social
tweets.translated: RT @cz_binance：发现了一些 bánh mì 吗？ 🥖 我们知道那个样子。 😋 如果您在越南旅行，好消息是：您可以使用加密货币付款。只需使用 Binance Pay 扫描本地二维码即可外带食物。具体方法如下 → https://www.binance.com/events/scan-and-pay-tourist- payment?utm_source=social

tweets.content: Looks like there's a high power cylindrical cell / BBU cell shortage (aka: bottleneck): - Samsung SDI supplies the cells to Simplo, which assembles them into BBUs for $META to $AMZN. - As demand from data centers has increased, production of Samsung SDI's cylindrical cells has also risen sharply. - Industry sources said Panasonic, Samsung are experiencing supply shortages for BBU cells. So main beneficiaries imo look like: - Samsung SDI (KRX: 006400) / Panasonic Energy as cleaneset winners - Murata (6981) / this keeps showing up everywhere with MLCC and others lol - LG Energy (KRX: 373220) / incoming There's not really much direct US players? But adjacent read through like $VRT, $ETN or $BWA / $ENS. Not quite in my domain emphasis cup of tea though. I wouldn't conflate this as all shortages having a massive TAM, but there could be opportunities...
tweets.translated: 看起来存在高功率圆柱形电池/BBU 电池短缺（又名：瓶颈）： - 三星 SDI 向 Simplo 供应电池，Simplo 将它们组装成 BBU，价格为 $META 到 $AMZN。 - 随着数据中心需求的增加，三星SDI圆柱形电池的产量也急剧增加。 - 业内消息人士称，松下、三星正面临 BBU 电池供应短缺的问题。因此，在我看来，主要受益者是这样的： - 三星 SDI (KRX: 006400) / 松下能源 (Panasonic Energy) 作为清洁集赢家 - 村田制作所 (6981) / MLCC 和其他产品随处可见，哈哈 - LG 能源 (KRX: 373220) / 传入 美国没有太多直接参与者吗？但相邻的通读例如 $VRT、$ETN 或 $BWA / $ENS。不过，这并不完全属于我所强调的领域。我不会将其混为一谈，因为所有短缺都具有大量 TAM，但可能存在机会......

tweets.content: Leaked Anthropic docs show plans to secure 1.4GW capacity from Australia, amounting to ~$21.6B. Recently $IREN, $SHAZ, and other Neoclouds have been building sovereign DCs in Australia. Guess like a lot of things are stating to make more sense connection wise? (disclosure: no open positions in any of the above).
tweets.translated: 泄露的 Anthropic 文档显示，计划从澳大利亚获得 1.4GW 产能，价值约 $21.6B。最近，$IREN、$SHAZ 和其他 Neoclouds 一直在澳大利亚建立主权 DC。我猜想很多事情都在说是为了让联系更有意义？ （披露：上述任何一项均无未平仓头寸）。

tweets.content: Nikkei - $JD E-Commerce Giant CEO warns "Robots Will Replace 700,000 Delivery Workers". Per FT's earlier piece last month: The 700,000 delivery personnel working for the firm will be replaced by robots, highlighting how rapid automation has become a new threat to China's already severe job market. Liu Qiangdong, revealed that the company has signed contracts with approximately 120 schools to retrain its delivery workers, enabling them to transition into new roles such as robot repair and maintenance... "Beijing has started to track AI’s hit to jobs as a national priority." This follows other corporate strategy reports like $AMZN, of avoiding 600,000 future hires with robots. But it's very interesting to see the shift in worker roles to robot support. Maybe $DASH to $UBER to $MELI, we'll all start to see this rollout following China. There's a lot of retail disbelief over humanoid/robotics commercialization. But feels like the direction industry is heading down is pretty clear.
tweets.translated: 日经新闻 - $JD 电子商务巨头首席执行官警告“机器人将取代 70 万送货工人”。英国《金融时报》上个月早些时候发表的文章称：该公司的 70 万名送货人员将被机器人取代，这突显出快速的自动化已成为中国本已严峻的就业市场的新威胁。刘强东透露，该公司已与大约120所学校签订了合同，对送货员进行再培训，使他们能够转型到机器人维修和保养等新岗位……“北京已开始将追踪人工智能对就业的影响作为国家优先事项。”在此之前，其他公司战略报告（例如 $AMZN）报告称，未来将避免 60 万名机器人员工被聘用。但看到工人角色向机器人支持的转变是非常有趣的。也许 $DASH 到 $UBER 到 $MELI，我们都会在中国之后开始看到这一推出。许多零售业者对人形/机器人商业化持怀疑态度。但感觉行业的下行方向非常明确。
